commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add splitFaceByPolygon kernel function

Implements the core polygon-split algorithm that all drawing-on-face
tools (Rectangle, Circle, Arc, Polygon) will use. For each polygon edge,
extends it to an infinite line and splits all tracked faces sequentially,
then identifies the inner face by centroid proximity.

Also fixes a critical bug in splitFaceByLine where the t parameter passed
to splitEdge was not adjusted for shared edges whose stored half-edge
direction is opposite to the face's half-edge direction. This caused
splits on box faces (or any face with shared edges) to create vertices
at the wrong positions (1-t instead of t).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

commit.message:
Fix Vznikne→Vynikne easter egg scroll trigger

Previous offset assumed section would traverse the full viewport,
but as the last section on the page it never reached the 0.35
threshold. New offset tracks a realistic range for bottom-of-page
content and triggers the z→y swap earlier for better visibility.

feat(web): support browser Back/Forward for same-route UI state transitions (#171)

## Summary
- Add `useHistoryState` hook that syncs React state with browser history
via `pushState`/`popstate`
- Integrate into Home page (view/greenhouse selection),
GreenhouseMapPage (map/register/waitlist), and AdminDashboard (tab
navigation)
- Pressing Back after a same-route state change now returns to the
previous UI state instead of leaving the page

## Test plan
- [x] 10 new unit tests for `useHistoryState` hook covering: initial
values, pushState on change, popstate restore, duplicate entry
prevention, multi-key coexistence, object values, and full multi-step
Back/Forward cycle
- [x] All 490 existing tests pass (shared: 82, api: 284, web: 124)
- [x] Lint clean, build succeeds
- [ ] Manual testing: navigate greenhouse map -> register -> press Back
-> verify return to map view
- [ ] Manual testing: switch admin tabs -> press Back -> verify return
to previous tab

Closes #165

commit.message:
Fix mobile RTL math direction + underbrace garbling

- CSS: direction:ltr on mjx-container — MathJax inherits dir:rtl from
  the page on mobile, reversing xrightarrow formulas (Purim → formula
  was showing backwards). Math is always LTR convention.
- Article: \underbrace → \underset for P≠NP formula — \underbrace with
  Hebrew text renders as garbled boxes on mobile. \underset places the
  label below without a bracket and renders correctly.

commit.message:
Add json module to stdlib with self-contained parser

Built-in JSON parser (no serde_json dependency) for edge/WASM compatibility.
Supports: parse, stringify, get, get_string, get_int, get_bool, get_array,
keys, to_string, to_int, from_string, from_int, from_bool, null, array,
from_map. All targets (Rust, TS, JS). Requires import json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive plant library with 50 plants, soil & fertilizer data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add FixedStrideRouting and CrossAttentionDeTokenizer

FixedStrideRouting: deterministic every-Nth-position boundaries,
bypassing learned routing entirely.

CrossAttentionDeTokenizer: each full-resolution position attends to
all compressed tokens via cross-attention, replacing EMA's
sequential broadcast. Drop-in replacement for DeTokenizer.

Both configurable via --routing=fixed_stride and
--detokenizer=cross_attention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: nanoGPT
repository.description: The simplest, fastest repository for training/finetuning medium-sized GPTs.

commit.message:
fix(auth): harden multi-user security with PKCE, session binding, and strict IP check

- Verify PKCE S256 code_verifier in exchangeAuthorizationCode (was bypassed entirely)
- Bind MCP sessions to authenticated user's Notion token (prevents session hijack)
- Remove raw ntn_/secret_ token pass-through in HTTP mode (must go through OAuth)
- Require both IPs known and matching for pending bind claims (strict IP check)
- Bind auth codes to initiating client_id (prevents cross-client code theft)
- Reduce pending bind TTL from 2min to 30s

commit.message:
Separate neighbors and airNeighbors into mutually exclusive sets

Cross-island and line-of-sight connections are now stored only in
`airNeighbors`, never in `neighbors`. The `neighbors` array contains
exclusively hex-grid land connections from the Go output. The painter
draws each type in a separate pass, gated by independent flags. A new
"show air connections" checkbox is added to the demo UI.

commit.message:
fix: use merged 24h CSV cache for fire data instead of single API result

Prevents fire count inconsistency (80 vs 288) caused by VIIRS satellite
pass timing — loadFiresCache() merges all 24h CSV files by ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use Node.js 20 from NodeSource for ESM compatibility

Debian bookworm's Node.js 18 has CJS/ESM compat issues with render-glb's
jsdom dependency chain. NodeSource Node.js 20 resolves this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: filter steam discovery to games only via appmanifest ACF files

Replace naive steamapps/common/ directory scan with appmanifest_*.acf
parsing. Only entries declaring type = "Game" are included, which
filters out Proton runtimes, SteamLinuxRuntime, and other tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set pairCreatedAt=Date.now() for Jupiter-resolved tokens

Jupiter Token API doesn't return pair creation time, so the age filter
had nothing to check. Since these tokens come from webhooks/WebSocket
at pool creation time, Date.now() is the correct creation timestamp.

commit.message:
feat: wire voice tasks to @tasks database + code conversation pickup

- Create lib/notion-tasks.js: creates tasks directly in the @tasks
  Notion database with proper status, priority, owner, due date, and
  task type fields. Includes natural-language date parsing and task
  type inference from content keywords.
- Update voice.js: route notion_task intent through create_task()
  instead of create_capture(), passing due_date and task_type.
  Replace code_conversation stub with real handler that confirms
  the prompt is queued for Claude Code pickup.
- Update intent.js: add task_type and urgent priority to the intent
  schema so Claude can categorize tasks during voice classification.
- Add code_conversation_queued() TTS response.
- Add NOTION_TASKS_DB_ID to .env.example.

feat(web): redesign friends/achievements/stats — violet theme + fix API routing

- Error state now shows correctly when auth fails

- Full violet theme rewrite (no DaisyUI classes)
- Replace apiClient with authFetch helper (reads token from localStorage)
- Add custom tab bar with violet active state
- Create proxy routes: /api/users/friends, /api/users/friends/requests,
  /api/users/friends/accept/[id], /api/users/search

Achievements:
- Full violet theme rewrite
- Send auth token in fetch; correct endpoint /api/achievements/me
- Rarity colors: common=zinc, rare=blue, epic=violet, legendary=amber
- Clean modal with backdrop blur

pipeline via enhanced_review.rs.
